name: Community Legal Services Of Mid-Florida, Inc. - Orlando Office
description_short: Community Legal Services provides no-cost legal assistance and education to low-income individuals across Florida, with offices including Orlando, covering family law, housing, financial matters, and consumer rights.
description_long:
Community Legal Services of Mid-Florida has been operating for 60 years, establishing itself as a established non-profit legal services provider serving low-income and vulnerable populations across Florida. The organization operates multiple office locations, including the Orlando office, and delivers comprehensive legal support without cost to eligible clients. Their mission centers on enhancing lives through legal support, assistance, and education for those who cannot afford traditional legal representation.

The organization offers a broad range of legal services including family law (divorce and children's issues), bankruptcy assistance, housing and fair housing advocacy, financial matters counseling, small business and non-profit support, disaster relief legal aid, SNAP/food assistance navigation, student and children's rights protection, and domestic violence protection services. They also provide specialized support for older adults and conduct public legal education through clinic events. Services are delivered both in-person at physical office locations and through online virtual clinics.

CLS distinguishes itself through its 60-year track record, multi-county service area (evidenced by locations in Daytona Beach and references to Volusia County), and diverse practice areas that extend beyond basic legal aid into specialized areas like small business support and disaster relief. Their hybrid service model combining physical clinics with online legal clinics increases accessibility for people unable to travel.

The primary limitation is that this is a non-profit with likely eligibility requirements based on income level, and service availability may be constrained by funding and capacity. The website does not clearly display eligibility criteria, service coverage areas for the Orlando office specifically, intake procedures, or current case acceptance status. Individuals should verify eligibility and contact the Orlando office directly before assuming they qualify for services.
pros: No-cost legal services—completely free representation and assistance for eligible clients
60-year established non-profit organization with deep community roots and institutional stability
Comprehensive service areas including family law, bankruptcy, housing, financial matters, and small business support
Accessible through multiple delivery formats: in-person clinics and online virtual clinics
Specialized support for vulnerable populations including older adults, children, domestic violence survivors, and disaster victims
Multiple office locations across Florida expanding geographic accessibility
Regular legal clinic events (documented upcoming April 2026 clinics for family law and bankruptcy)
cons: No published eligibility income limits or asset thresholds on the website—potential applicants cannot self-screen
Orlando office address and phone contact information not displayed on the website despite being listed as a specific location
No information about current case acceptance status or wait times for intake appointments
Service coverage areas not clearly defined for the Orlando office specifically
Website lacks intake procedures, required documentation, or application process details
best_for: Low-income individuals and families needing divorce, custody, or family law representation
Bankruptcy filers who cannot afford attorney fees for Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 filing
Renters and homeowners facing housing discrimination, eviction, or fair housing violations
Small business owners and non-profit organizations needing formation and compliance legal guidance
diagnosis: Community Legal Services is best for low-income Florida residents who need affordable or free legal help with family matters, housing, bankruptcy, or consumer rights issues. The major caveat is that eligibility requirements are not published online, so interested parties must contact the organization directly to confirm they qualify before investing time in an intake process.
services: Family law assistance (divorce and children-related matters)
Bankruptcy legal representation and filing support
Fair housing advocacy and discrimination defense
Housing legal assistance (eviction defense, landlord disputes, habitability issues)
Financial matters counseling and debt-related legal advice
Small business formation and non-profit legal services
Disaster relief legal aid and assistance
SNAP and food assistance application support and appeals
Older adult legal rights protection and planning
Student and children's rights advocacy and education
Online and in-person legal clinic consultations
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1440 N Nova Rd, Suite 101, Daytona Beach, FL 32117
